chapters.summary: An unidentified speaker makes a remark about attendance before the hearing officially begins.
chapters.title: Sergeant-at-arms opens the hearing

utterances.speaker: UNKNOWN
utterances.text: If she's not counted for attendance, we're all coming for you.

chapters.summary: William Martin welcomes attendees to the Committee on Aging vote, asks everyone to silence electronic devices, and reminds the public not to approach the dais.
chapters.title: Committee clerk William Martin gives housekeeping instructions

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Good morning and welcome to today's New York City Council vote for the Committee on Aging.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: At this time, please silence all electronic devices.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: And just a reminder, no one may approach the dais at any time.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Chair, we are ready to begin.

chapters.summary: Gale Brewer explains she is chairing in place of Council Member Zhuang, recognizes present members including Dinowitz, Schulman, Hudson, Aldebol, and notes Zhuang was present earlier.
chapters.title: Acting Chair Brewer introduces herself and recognizes attending members

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I'm Gail Brewer.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I will be chairing today's Committee on Aging vote in place of Council Members Wang, who couldn't be here today.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: She was on earlier, so I want to say thank you for that.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Before we begin, I want to recognize the council members who are here.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Dinowitz, Schulman, Hudson, Aldeball, and Zhuang, like I said, was here earlier.

chapters.summary: Brewer explains that Intro 812, sponsored by Council Member Carcione, would require the Commissioner of Aging to develop a protocol with NYC Emergency Management to conduct wellness checks during cold weather alerts, extreme heat warnings, and heat-related emergencies.
chapters.title: Brewer introduces Intro 812 on wellness checks for older adults during extreme weather

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: We'll be voting on Intro 812, sponsored by a council member in Carcione.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: The bill would require the Commissioner of Aging to develop a protocol in collaboration with the New York City Emergency Management and other relevant agencies

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: to conduct wellness checks in the event of cold weather alerts, extreme heat warnings, and heat-related emergencies.

chapters.summary: Brewer details that wellness checks would include questions about an older adult's well-being, temperature conditions, functionality of climate control equipment, and availability of food and water.
chapters.title: Brewer describes wellness check protocol details

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: These wellness checks would include a set of questions developed by aging to gauge an older adult's well-being and what the conditions are like in that older adult's place of residence,

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: such as the temperature, the functionality of climate control equipment, and whether food and water is available.

chapters.summary: Brewer notes that older adults are more vulnerable to extreme heat and cold, which can disrupt access to needed care and aggravate pre-existing health conditions.
chapters.title: Brewer discusses vulnerability of older adults to extreme weather

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Older adults, that's me, are often more vulnerable to extreme hot and cold weather.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I survived.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Extreme heat or cold can disrupt access to the...

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: report that older adults need, it can also aggravate pre-existing health conditions.

chapters.summary: Brewer reports that 500 New Yorkers died prematurely from preventable heat illnesses last year, and warns that climate change will increase extreme heat days, noting three heat advisories already issued this year.
chapters.title: Brewer cites heat mortality statistics and climate change concerns

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Last year, according to emergency management, 500 New Yorkers died prematurely due to preventable heat illnesses.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: As climate change effects are felt in our city, the number of extreme heat days will only continue to rise.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: The city has already issued three extreme heat advisories this year, one of which was earlier this week.

chapters.summary: Brewer recalls the extreme heat over the 4th of July weekend and the extreme cold during winter, urging the Department of Aging to be proactive during extreme weather events to protect older New Yorkers.
chapters.title: Brewer references recent extreme weather events and calls for city preparedness

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: We will all remember how hot it was on the 4th of July weekend.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: This is all on the back of extreme cold that gripped our city during the winter.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: The city must be prepared.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: And aging, the department must be proactive during extreme weather events to protect older New Yorkers.

chapters.summary: Brewer thanks Council Member Carcione for her leadership, the Speaker and colleagues for supporting the legislation, and staff and advocates for their hard work, then yields to the clerk for the roll call vote.
chapters.title: Brewer thanks sponsor, Speaker, colleagues, staff, and advocates

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to thank Councilmember Carcion for her leadership on this issue.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to thank the Speaker and my colleagues for supporting this legislation.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I want to thank the staff and advocates for their hard work.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: I pass this issue to the clerk to call the roll.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you.

chapters.summary: William Martin calls the roll for the Committee on Aging vote on Intro 812. Brewer, Dinowitz, Hudson, Shulman, and Aldebol vote in the affirmative.
chapters.title: Committee clerk conducts roll call vote on Intro 812

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Good morning.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: William Martin, committee clerk, roll call vote committee on aging.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Acting chair Brewer.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Dinowitz.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Yes.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Hudson.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Mealy.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Shulman.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Aye.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Aldebol.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Aye.

chapters.summary: Martin announces the item has been approved with five votes in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions.
chapters.title: Committee approves Intro 812 with initial vote of 5-0

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: With a vote of five in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions, item has been approved by the committee.

chapters.summary: Council Member Joanne's vote is recorded, bringing the final tally to six in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstentions. Brewer then closes the hearing.
chapters.title: Council Member Joanne's vote recorded, final tally 6-0

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Councilmember Joanne?

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Thank you, Councilmember Joann.

utterances.speaker: William Martin
utterances.text: Vote is now six in the affirmative, zero in the negative, and no abstention.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: Thank you.

utterances.speaker: Gale A. Brewer
utterances.text: This hearing is closed.

summary: The Committee on Aging, chaired by Gale Brewer in place of Chair Zhuang, voted on Intro 812-A, a bill requiring wellness checks on older adults during extreme weather events.

The bill mandates the Commissioner of Aging to develop a protocol with NYC Emergency Management to conduct wellness checks during cold weather alerts, extreme heat warnings, and heat-related emergencies.
The bill passed with six votes in the affirmative and zero in the negative.
title: Committee on Aging
topic: Wellness checks on older adults during certain weather events, and to repeal repetitions of the definition of “older adult”
